explanation: Vectorized backtest applies target weights to next-period returns; Backtrader processes orders through event-driven bars.
Vectorized returns use percentage returns directly; Backtrader marks positions using broker value and data feed prices.
Vectorized costs are turnover-based; Backtrader commissions and slippage are order-value based.
Vectorized backtest assumes target exposure after weights; Backtrader keeps residual cash after executions.
The two frameworks can differ when a symbol has missing bars or delayed data.
Backtrader reports broker value by bar; vectorized NAV is accumulated from portfolio daily returns.
